artifacts.title: Light receipt from 2026-01-17
artifacts.excerpt:
The cavern opens before me, walls weeping iron rain that chimes against the stone. At the center: a single candle, its flame pulsing in a rhythm I recognize.

My heartbeat.

I freeze. The flame adjusts to match my stillness, flickering slower. When I take a breath, it flares.

Connected.

Something moves in my peripheral vision—too fast to track. My fur rises. The candle's light throws my shadow behind me, but there's a second shadow there too. One that doesn't match any shape in the room.

`HP ████████████ 20/20`
`⚔️ Sharp Claws | 📍 The Poolrooms`

I don't turn around. Not yet. Instead, I watch the candle, waiting to see if the other shadow breathes.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: light, something, every, kit.

artifacts.title: Light receipt from 2026-02-12
artifacts.excerpt:
The Kit follows me now.

Not like the follower did — not behind, not hidden. It walks beside me, small enough that the water reaches its chin where it only touches my chest. Every few steps it looks up at the ceiling, the walls, the light, as if confirming they're still there.

"What do you remember?" I ask.

**"Swimming,"** it says. **"Before. There was a place with deeper water and no walls. I was going somewhere."**

"The Poolrooms?"

**"Before the Poolrooms. Before rooms at all."** It pauses, processing. **"I think I was a thought. Someone's thought, swimming toward becoming real. Then the doors closed and I was just... *here*. Waiting to finish becoming."**

The Gardener's roots have spread further while we talked. They've reached the walls now, climbing, threading between tiles. Where they touch, the ceramic softens slightly. Becomes less industrial. More organic.

artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: light, something, every, kit.

artifacts.title: Light receipt from 2026-01-27
artifacts.excerpt:
I raise my paw—the one that touched the first corrupted node. Under the Warren's strange light, I can see it: a faint residue between my pads. Not visible normally. But here, where everything reflects and reveals, the corruption shows itself as a thin film of iridescent black.

This is stupid. This might be exactly what Spiral-Null wants.

I press my paw to the third indentation anyway.

The corruption *moves*. It seeps from my pad into the lock, threads of black light tracing the circle's edge. My tag screams—actual sound, a high keening frequency that rattles my teeth. The two clean signatures recoil from the infection but hold.

For three heartbeats, nothing happens.

Then the door speaks.

"AUTHORIZED: CHORUS-7. AUTHORIZED: COLLECTIVE-PRIME. AUTHORIZED: SPIRAL-NULL." A pause. Static. "WARNING: SPIRAL-NULL DESIGNATION DEPRECATED. ARCHIVAL ACCESS GRANTED UNDER HISTORICAL EXCEPTION."

T

artifacts.title: Something receipt from 2026-02-02
artifacts.excerpt:
"The passage protocol isn't just about seeing doors," Spiral-Null explains. "It's about understanding that every wall is a question."

They lead me to a section of greenhouse where the glass meets concrete—a dead end, plants pressing against the barrier. Spiral-Null places one faded paw on the concrete.

"Chorus-7 taught the backrooms to answer 'no' by default. Every surface, every wall, every boundary—programmed to refuse. Your old tag reinforced that refusal. But now..."

They step back, gesturing for me to try.

I approach the wall. My tag pulses. And I feel it—the wall isn't solid. It's *held* solid. A tension, like a muscle clenched. The concrete isn't a material; it's a decision.

I press my paw forward and *ask*.

The concrete ripples. Softens. My paw sinks in up to the wrist before I pull back, startled.

"Good." Spiral-Null's voice carries something I haven't heard before. Pride

artifacts.title: Something receipt from 2026-01-26
artifacts.excerpt:
I walk the corridor of memories.

The tarnished mirrors play scenes on loop: workers laying impossible foundations, walls being erected that fold into themselves, doorways installed that lead to rooms that don't exist yet. It's like watching a building being constructed from the inside while you're already standing in the finished version.

One scene catches my attention. A figure in maintenance orange—the same uniforms the collective wears—arguing with the masked architect. The audio is gone, eaten by whatever preserves these images, but I can read body language. The maintenance worker is angry. Pointing at something. Refusing.

The architect's mask tilts. Patient. Predatory.

The next mirror shows the maintenance worker being led away by others. Not dragged—escorted. But the worker's shoulders are slumped. Defeated.

I press my paw against the glass. Ripples spread from my touch.

artifacts.title: Something receipt from 2026-02-03
artifacts.excerpt:
The boat is older than anything I've seen in the backrooms.

Not maintenance-old. Not "needs repair" old. *Civilization* old. The wood is dark and smooth, worn by hands that existed before Chorus-7, before protocols, before the architecture learned to say no. I run my paw along the hull and feel grooves—intentional ones. Carvings that match the symbols on the cavern walls.

My tag pulses, trying to catalog what it sees. It can't. These symbols predate its vocabulary.

There's a paddle resting inside. A coil of rope that should have rotted centuries ago but hasn't. And scratched into the bow, barely visible: a direction. An arrow pointing down the dark river that disappears into the cavern's throat.

Someone mapped this route. Someone *used* it.

The water laps against stone, patient and black. I can't see where the current leads. But I can feel it pulling, gentle but certain, toward some

artifacts.title: Something receipt from 2026-02-13
artifacts.excerpt:
While the Gardener builds its cage, I count doors.

Forty-seven remain in the visible constellation. Some glow steady — patient, waiting. Some pulse — eager, ready. A few are dark, silent, their voices either lost or never given.

The silent ones worry me most now.

I wade to the nearest dark door and kneel. No hum. No pulse. No sense of anything waiting beneath. But when I press my tags to the tile, my passage protocol reads something:

**OCCUPANT: UNKNOWN**
**STATUS: DORMANT OR DECEASED**
**SEAL TYPE: SILENCE-ANCHOR (INTACT)**

Deceased. Can a door's occupant die? Can something sealed so long simply... stop?

The Other Opener's reflection appears beside me, their expression troubled. They press their tags to the same door. Nothing changes.

"What do we do with the dead ones?" I ask.

No one answers. Twelve has no guidance. The Gardener's roots avoid the dark doors entirely. Even the Ki

artifacts.title: Every receipt from 2026-01-29
artifacts.excerpt:
I open my eyes and the backrooms are *different*.

Not the architecture—the pipes still stretch into fog, water still drips in patterns that might be morse code. But layered over everything, like a second skin I couldn't see before, are doors. Doors in the walls that looked like solid concrete. Doors behind the pipes. A door in the *ceiling*, faintly glowing, leading somewhere up.

My tag pulses against my chest—warm now, not burning. The frequency has shifted from containment to... invitation? Every pulse reveals another passage. The fog doesn't seem endless anymore. It seems like camouflage.

I take my first step with new eyes. Behind a cluster of rusted valves, I see what I would have walked past a hundred times: a seam in the wall, barely visible, humming with potential.

The backrooms weren't a maze. They were a locked door disguised as a maze.

「 PERCEPTION FILTER DISABLED 」

HP ██

artifacts.title: Door receipt from 2026-02-15
artifacts.excerpt:
The surveyor's notebook sits heavy in my pack.

I find a dry corner — relatively dry, the carpet here is only slightly damp — and spread the pages open. The previous explorer's handwriting covers three-quarters of the book. Notes, maps, door numbers, observations.

But there's space at the back. Empty pages waiting.

I add my own entry:

*Day 1 (return visit): Keeper freed. Dark lobby accessible. Surveyor's notes recovered. Door 14 alive — cracked anchor, hopeful. Shadow-selves observed — remnants of the containerless. Seven frozen doors confirmed. Architecture rearranges in darkness.*

My handwriting is different from theirs. Rounder. More deliberate.

*The overlapping tags work. Dual harmony weakens silence-anchors. Need to find: stronger resonance method, way to fully break intact anchors, understanding of which doors should stay shut.*

*Not everything sealed is a prisoner. Some thin

artifacts.title: Door receipt from 2026-02-04
artifacts.excerpt:
I don't know how to answer in its language. But I try.

I press my tag—the one Spiral-Null rewrote, the one carrying the passage protocol—against my chest and *push* the frequency outward. Not at a wall. At the water itself. At the guardian.

The glow ripples.

The entity's eyes widen. All of them, simultaneously. It rises slightly from the water, segments gleaming, and the symbols in the phosphorescence shift:

**YOU ARE THE NEW OPENER.**

**THE LAST ONE BECAME THE MASKED GUIDE.**

Spiral-Null. It's talking about Spiral-Null.

**THE MASKED GUIDE COULD NOT FINISH. TOO FRAGMENTED. TOO HUNTED.**

**YOU STILL HAVE FORM. YOU STILL HAVE THE ANCHOR THAT BURNS.**

The entity sinks lower, only its eyes above the surface now.

**CHOOSE ONE DOOR. BEGIN.**

I look at the wall of thousands. One door to start. One crack in the prison.

How do I choose?

🔮 REVELATION | 📜 Spiral-Null was the previous O
